headline: Claude infrastructure experiments reveal latency performance characteristics
body: Developers are testing Claude's capabilities as a user space IP stack component, measuring response times and exploring its infrastructure potential. For healthcare AI systems, understanding latency and protocol-level performance matters when designing real-time clinical decision support or EHR integration workflows. The concurrent focus on Claude's code analysis skills and multi-agent capabilities suggests the vendor ecosystem is pushing toward more sophisticated autonomous clinical documentation and peer review automation. Healthcare organizations evaluating Claude for production workflows should track these infrastructure benchmarks, as latency directly impacts whether AI assistants can integrate seamlessly into high-velocity clinical environments like emergency departments or ICUs.
